steps.html: Transforming Iterate into a new Interpreter into a gamified environment...
...to pitching at four consecutive Microsoft Hackathons...
...to open-sourcing the whole stack from Unity to Azure;

intro.summary: BitNaughts isn't just an educational programming video-game: it's code gamified!

items.NAME: ☄️ BitNaughts
items.TEXT:
Parent Repository & Submodule Orchestrator
BitNaughts is an educational programming video game that teaches players coding concepts through interactive gameplay. The parent repo ties together the full project via Git submodules — linking bitnaughts.unity (game client); bitnaughts.github.io (WebGL front-end); and bitnaughts.mainframe (cloud back-end).

items.NAME: 🎮 Unity
items.TITLE: Unity 6.0
items.TEXT:
Unity Game Client
The core game application built in Unity 6.0 (100% C#). Players navigate a spaceship; dock at planets; and solve programming challenges through an in-game terminal that interprets assembly-style instructions.

Submodule Architecture:
  • Assets — Sprites; audio; and visual assets
  • UI/UX — Interface design and user experience components
  • Components — Modular game systems (navigation; docking; inventory)
  • C# Interpreter — The embedded language interpreter engine
  • WebGLNativeInputField — Custom input handling for WebGL builds
Contributors: 4 (Mutilar; botnaughts; vva0; nt314p)

items.NAME: 👨‍💻 Interpreter
items.TITLE: C#
items.MOTTO: Stepping through each OP Code of abstracted languages
items.TEXT:
Assembly Interpreter Engine
The core language processing engine written in C# that powers BitNaughts' in-game programming challenges. Players write assembly-level OP codes which this interpreter steps through instruction-by-instruction.

Architecture:
  • Interpreter.cs — Main execution loop; parses and executes OP code instructions sequentially
  • Instruction.cs — Data model representing individual assembly instructions (opcode + operands)
  • Variable.cs — Register and memory variable management for the virtual machine
  • ColorCoder.cs — Syntax highlighting for the in-game terminal; color-codes keywords; registers; and literals
Designed as a Git submodule consumed by bitnaughts.unity; enabling modular development of the language engine independent of the game client.

items.NAME: 📺 bitnaughts.io
items.TITLE: WebGL
items.MOTTO: Play the demo in your browser!
items.TEXT:
WebGL Front-End — bitnaughts.io
The web-hosted runtime that serves Unity WebGL builds directly in the browser.

Pages:
  • play.html — Primary single-player game interface
  • duo.html — Multiplayer / co-op mode
  • alpha.html — Alpha testing build
  • portal.html — Landing page and navigation portal
Key Directories:
  • Build/ — Compiled Unity WebGL artifacts (.unityweb; .json; UnityLoader.js)
  • StreamingAssets/ — Runtime data files loaded by Unity at play-time
  • TemplateData/ — Loading screen and progress bar assets
  • assets/ — Static images and media
  • documentation/ — Project docs and guides

items.NAME: 🎤 Voice
items.TITLE: Narrator
items.TEXT:
A.I. Voice Narration — TorToiSe TTS
A fork of neonbjb/tortoise-tts adapted for in-game AI narration. TorToiSe is a multi-voice text-to-speech system that uses an autoregressive decoder paired with a diffusion model to generate highly realistic speech.

Capabilities:
  • Multi-voice synthesis — clone and generate speech in different character voices from short reference clips
  • High-quality prosody and intonation via autoregressive sequence modeling
  • Diffusion-based audio refinement for natural-sounding output
  • Configurable voice presets for distinct in-game characters and narrators
Use Case: Generate dynamic AI narration for BitNaughts' tutorial sequences; mission briefings; and in-game lore — replacing static audio recordings with procedurally generated speech.
License: Apache-2.0

items.NAME: 📡 Mainframe
items.TITLE: Azure
items.TEXT:
Serverless Back-End — bitnaughts.azurewebsites.net
An Azure Function App written in C# that provides cloud persistence for multiplayer game state. Hosted as a serverless endpoint with two HTTP-triggered functions.

Functions:
  • Ping() — Multiplayer state sync via MongoDB; upserts player data keyed by name; returns all other players' state (uses ♖ delimiter). Connection secured with TLS 1.2 and TTL indexes for automatic document expiry (expireAfterSeconds: 10).
  • Mainframe() — Git-based persistence layer; clones bitnaughts.db repo to temp storage; reads/writes player save files as .txt; then commits and pushes changes back via LibGit2Sharp.
Stack: Azure Functions; MongoDB (CosmosDB-compatible); LibGit2Sharp; Newtonsoft.Json
